MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ray Robinson for trustee Windy Cherry (via Zoom).
Clinton Killian for objectors Jimmy Cherry, Jr. and Delphine Cherry (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Status of Opening Blocked Accounts.

Mr. Robinson reported that the net proceeds deposited into the blocked account totaled $35,221.90 rather than the amount previously anticipated, explaining that an older redevelopment lien resulted in a substantially higher payoff than originally estimated. Mr. Robinson stated that declarations and supporting exhibits had been filed reflecting the revised figures.

Following discussion, the Court directed Mr. Robinson to file an ex parte petition to amend the prior order to reflect the actual amount deposited. The Court continued the matter to March 10, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and advised that if the amended order is obtained prior to the hearing, the matter may be taken off calendar.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Off Calendar Nov-12-2025 Continued To Jan-21-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ray Robinson for trustee Windy Cherry (via Zoom).
Clinton Killian for objectors Jimmy Cherry, Jr. and Delphine Cherry (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Status of Opening Blocked Accounts.

The Court disclosed that it had previously represented Mr. Killian years ago.

The Court inquired about the status of the blocked account receipt. Mr. Robinson explained that escrow had been delayed due to complications with the sale of the trust property. He reported that the City and County of San Francisco holds a redevelopment loan dating back to the 1990s, originally in the amount of approximately $80,000, on which no payments have been made for decades. As of October, the accumulated interest had grown to approximately $937,000.

Mr. Robinson stated that he has been negotiating with the City to reduce the outstanding balance and resolve associated tax issues. He further explained that the City requires the property to be marketed for 90 days to low-income prospective buyers before granting a release of its right of first refusal, which has contributed to the delays. The title company has been provided a copy of the Court's prior order requiring that any remaining proceeds from the sale be wired into a blocked account. The buyer is currently waiting for the City's process to conclude, and closing is anticipated around December 10, 2025, assuming no further action by the City.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to January 21, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
